title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
Hakaste and the narrator discuss the investigation into a murder at Pema's house. Hakaste suspects Hillary Weber, who discovered the body, noting that her lunch break aligns with the estimated time of death. The narrator questions the suspicion of Weber, given her apparent distress, and notes that Pema is a blind woman who worked as a math teacher before finding employment at the Allenberg Institute. Hakaste reveals that the investigation into a man named Kirby has yielded nothing; his business card, name, address, and the Big City Small Town Insurance Company appear to be entirely fabricated. The autopsy indicates the victim was killed with a sharp instrument, such as a kitchen knife.

The investigators consider seeking clues from neighbors in the upper-class residential area of Wilbraham Crescent. Hakaste notes that number 18 is occupied by Mr. Waitehouse, an assistant at a law firm, and his sister, Edith. At number 18, James Waitehouse expresses concern to his sister about recent suspicious activity in the neighborhood, but Edith dismisses his fears as sacrilege. Edith discusses rumors that the victim may have been an accountant from Allerton College investigating financial discrepancies. She defends Miss Pembroke’s character, rejecting the theory that she strangled the man. After James leaves for work, Edith, feeling agitated by the possibility of danger, places a nine-iron from her golf bag near the front door for protection.
